title: The reflecting pi-context CLI has no op to project a config-declared target-lens as a binned item-view (the lens-view primitive exists but is reachable only inside a roadmap)
description: FILED AT THE GENERAL CLASS ALTITUDE per the gap-explore-surfaces-class convention. Triggering instance (experience gap, surfaced by dogfooding the CLI): wanting to 'see open gaps', config.lenses[] declares a gaps-by-status target-lens (target framework-gaps, derived_from_field status, bins identified/accepted/in-progress/closed/wontfix) but the CLI has no op that renders it as a binned item-view. The GENERAL gap: the reflecting CLI has no op to project ANY config-declared lens as a binned view — it covers all four declared target-lenses (gaps-by-status, gaps-by-priority, tasks-by-status, features-by-status) and, since the underlying primitive is lens-kind-agnostic, also kind=composition and hand-curated-edge lenses; i.e. the entire config.lenses[] registry is unreachable as a queryable view surface. ROOT CAUSE — exists-but-unexposed, NOT not-implemented: the binned-view primitives are fully implemented in packages/pi-context/src/lens-view.ts — loadLensView(cwd, lensId) (lens-view.ts:43-97) resolves a named lens, reads its target block's items, computes edges, and groups items into the lens's declared bins (handles both kind:target and kind:composition); renderLensView(view, naming) (lens-view.ts:105-150) renders that as markdown (bins -> headings, items -> id/status/title lines) — exactly the 'open gaps binned by status' rendering wanted. But neither is reflected as a standalone op. The op-registry's only lensId-keyed op is context-edges-for-lens (ops-registry.ts:1795-1810), whose handler returns flat Edge[] ({parent:<bin>, child:<itemId>, relation_type}, paginated, no item bodies, no grouping) — edges, NOT the view. loadLensView/renderLensView are reached ONLY embedded in a roadmap: context-roadmap-load/render (ops-registry.ts:1901-1944) materialize per-phase lens-views via loadRoadmap -> serializeRoadmapView (keyed by roadmapId, reading roadmap.json phases). There is no path to render a standalone config.lenses[] entry by its lensId. SUPER-CLASS NOTE (not filed at this altitude, per gap-explore-surfaces-class rule 2 — too diffuse for one FGAP): this gap and FEAT-004's state-derivation gap are both members of a broader 'config-declared vocabulary (lenses / status_buckets / invariants / state-derivation) is under-surfaced as queryable CLI ops' class; lenses/invariants/status_buckets/state-derivation have distinct primitives + distinct fixes (FEAT-004 already owns the state-derivation member), so the right-sized coherent unit is the lens-view-op class, not the super-class. FEAT-004 (config-driven context-current-state/status) is adjacent but does NOT cover lens projection.

evidence.reference: loadLensView(cwd, lensId) — resolves config.lenses[] entry, reads target items, groups into declared bins (kind:target lines 76-96, kind:composition 61-74); returns {lens, items, edges, grouped, uncategorized, suggestionTemplate} — the binned view. Exists, fully capable of standalone use.
renderLensView(view, naming) — renders the loaded view as markdown (## <bin> headings, - **<id>** [<status>] — <title> lines). The 'see gaps binned by status' rendering.

context-edges-for-lens — the ONLY lensId-keyed op; returns flat Edge[] (bin-as-parent, id-as-child, paginated, no item bodies, no grouping) — NOT the view. Confirmed: context-edges-for-lens --lensId gaps-by-status --json -> 72 edge records, no titles/status.

context-roadmap-load/render — the ONLY reflected entry points to loadLensView/renderLensView, keyed by roadmapId (roadmap.json phases via serializeRoadmapView), not by a standalone config.lenses[] lensId. No op grep hit for context-lens-view / read-lens / view in the 54-op registry.

read-config --registry lenses --json: four target-lenses declared (gaps-by-status, gaps-by-priority, tasks-by-status, features-by-status) — all unreachable as a binned view; the workaround filter-block-items --block framework-gaps --field status --op eq --value '"identified"' returns one bin one status at a time (and over-cap data:null at totalBytes 56933), not the lens.

impact: The substrate declares a lens vocabulary + ships the binning/render primitives, but the reflecting CLI — the dogfooding + new-user surface — cannot answer 'show me <block> grouped by <status/priority>' for any declared lens. A caller wanting open gaps (or tasks-by-status, features-by-status) must hand-run filter-block-items one bin/one status-value at a time (and hit the 50KB read cap on framework-gaps), reconstructing by hand what loadLensView already computes. The lens model is a first-class config concept that is invisible at the CLI; every target-lens inherits the gap.
canonical_vocabulary: config-declared lens projected as a binned item-view via a reflected op
proposed_resolution: Add a reflected op (e.g. context-lens-view / read-lens) keyed by --lensId <id> that calls the existing loadLensView(cwd, lensId) (lens-view.ts:43) and returns the grouped item-view (bins -> item records with id/status/title), with the text/--format surface optionally rendering via the existing renderLensView (lens-view.ts:105). Serves ALL config.lenses[] entries (target, composition, hand-curated) since both primitives are lens-kind-agnostic. ADDITIVE: a new op reusing two shipped primitives; context-edges-for-lens + every other op byte-unchanged (consistent with the FEAT-008 additive invariant). The op<->CLI parity gate (parity-check.ts) auto-covers a newly-registered op; the per-op --help template (FGAP-072/TASK-042) auto-renders its synopsis/flags/examples. Route the view through structureForRead / the boundary read-cap (per FGAP-015) so a large lens view (framework-gaps already exceeds 50KB) fail-closes gracefully — the per-bin breakdown is itself the affordance to page by bin. Decompose per feature-decomposition when scheduled.
